page: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server instance featuring Intel Xeon 8375C processors. The system provides 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is a key component of this hardware profile, offering 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the i4i.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and PassMark CPU scores. The large-block read-only memory bandwidth also registers in the top 10%, reaching 338.82 GB/sec, while write bandwidth for large blocks is in the top 25% at 132.78 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50%. However, specialized workloads show strong results, with Geekbench ray tracing and image processing benchmarks both scoring in the top 10%.

From a resource density perspective, the i4i.metal offers a high ratio of local NVMe storage to memory and compute resources, making it a highly consolidated platform for data-intensive deployments. The bare-metal architecture eliminates hypervisor overhead, which aligns with the strong multi-core and database benchmark results, such as the PassMark database operations scoring in the top 25%. The primary tradeoff is the moderate single-core performance relative to the massive multi-core capacity. This server is configured for large-scale databases, high-throughput transactional applications, and data warehousing.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs, 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth, both ranking in the top 10%. Conversely, single-core performance remains in the middle 50%. The high density of local NVMe storage combined with bare-metal access provides a highly consolidated resource profile for data-intensive workloads. This server is suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput transactional systems, and data warehousing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 30,000 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, and 30,000 GB NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal server combining massive local NVMe storage, high-density memory, and top-tier multi-core performance for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and large-block memory read performance
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with bare-metal hardware access
Suited for large-scale databases and high-throughput transactional workloads
